L1.area: XML message processing and service communication layer

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) injection vulnerability exists where the XML parser used to process incoming service messages and external data does not disable external entity resolution or doctype declarations. An attacker who can control or influence the XML content being parsed can inject external entity references, potentially leading to disclosure of internal files, server-side request forgery, or denial of service.

L3.area: XML response verification and service metadata parsing in the digital signature client library
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) injection vulnerability exists in the XML parsing logic used to process digital signature service responses and service metadata retrieved from remote endpoints. The XML parser instances do not disable external entity resolution or doctype declarations before unmarshalling the content, allowing an attacker to inject crafted XML payloads that reference external entities. This can lead to disclosure of internal files, server-side request forgery, or denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The DocumentBuilderFactory instances used to parse XML sign responses and service metadata do not disable doctype declarations, allowing XML External Entity injection via crafted XML input.
